title: Release Freeze Exception Path
description: A release-and-rollout control workflow that manages promotion waves, rollback readiness, and deployment dependencies across environments. Focus case: release freeze exception path.
assumptions: Release instances are uniquely identifiable and immutable once pinned to an artifact_build.
Promotion waves and environment availability are represented by explicit availability flags consumed when assigned.
Approvals and notifications are modeled as discrete states (granted / withdrawn) and are necessary for exception progression.
On-call and incident rosters are pre-assigned and available to be referenced during the exception workflow.
Artifact pinning and cross-release coordination are required to prevent incompatible deployments during exception handling.
risks: Stale or revoked approvals delaying exception progression and causing stalled rollouts.
Unresolved dependency tickets leading to runtime failures after bypassing a freeze.
Inadequate monitoring profiles resulting in unnoticed regressions post-deployment.
Improper artifact pinning causing inconsistent deployments across promotion waves.
Human error in acknowledgements or notifications causing incorrect gating transitions.
Insufficient on-call coverage or misrouted incident responsibilities during rollback scenarios.
